programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: The Orbitrek X17 is the world's first fitness machine with 17 ever-changing pathways! Challenge your muscles as if running through different terrains in nature.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A corpse is discovered in a natural history museum, but the CSI detectives are unable to make an immediate identification because a swarm of flesh-eating beetles has partially devoured it. A six-year-old is later found hiding in the building, and proves to be a key witness. Meanwhile, Flack is called to a building site where a man has been poisoned with insulating foam

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Stella and Lindsay investigate the rape and murder of a hostess. Meanwhile, the life of a transplant patient hangs in the balance as Mac and the rest of the team try to find a hijacked helicopter carrying a donor liver. With Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Anna Belknap and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A man dressed in a superhero costume is found dead in an alley, and Mac, Stella and Dr Hawkes are taken aback to discover his everyday clothes in a nearby phone booth. Meanwhile, Danny and Lindsay investigate the murder of a promising NFL footballer. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Carmine Giovinazzo and Eddie Cahill

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for a top-secret CIA aircraft when two agents end up in Area 51 during a routine mission, while Walter plans to upload Megan's consciousness onto a hard drive

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion is blackmailed to break into the United Nations to assist in the assassination of a menacing arms dealer. Sylvester makes a bold move to protect Megan's wishes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for a top-secret CIA aircraft when two agents end up in Area 51 during a routine mission, while Walter plans to upload Megan's consciousness onto a hard drive

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion is blackmailed to break into the United Nations to assist in the assassination of a menacing arms dealer. Sylvester makes a bold move to protect Megan's wishes

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: On a live-fire air force training exercise, someone hacks into the control systems of a drone and fires one of its two live missiles, striking and killing a serviceman, before piloting it away. Sam and Callen meet with a member of the National Security Agency, while Eric's inquiries lead him to a group of students on an advanced computer science course and Kensi is sent under cover to pose as its guest speaker

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. At HQ, Hetty shows the team surveillance footage of the shooting and identifies the victim as Daniel Su, an engineer who defected from North Korea and was working for the US government on a highly classified research project. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Brandon Valdivia falls to his death from a rooftop bar and is initially thought to have committed suicide, but his family refuse to accept that possibility and persuade a congressman to put some pressure on the admiral. Sam and Callen are then brought in to re-evaluate the case and discover that Valdivia may have been involved in money laundering with a fellow marine.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.desc: Allison reinvestigates a 10-year-old murder case, and finds that the man convicted was innocent. Worse still, he was framed by somebody she knows. Paranormal drama, starring Patricia Arquette as a woman plagued by visions of the dead

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm and Mac go for a ride in a vintage aeroplane, but a malfunction forces them to land in a mountain clearing. As they languish miles away from civilisation, the pair are attacked by a gang of poachers who have killed a game warden. Drama, starring David James Elliott

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The death of the prime suspect in the murder case suggests there is a copycat killer on the loose. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The identity of the drug-dealer is discovered, and a new student acts strangely

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Natalia is arrested for the murder of her abusive ex-husband Nick Townsend, but the team is sidelined from the investigation by Internal Affairs, leaving it up to Horatio and Calleigh to prove her innocence. In a separate investigation, a wealthy single man is found dead in his mansion - and subsequent developments reveal the two cases are related

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A patrolman is murdered during a routine traffic stop, and Horatio and the team discover the vehicle used during the crime was driven by a 16-year-old student. The girl's mother continues to defend her, until events take a dramatic turn

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and his team investigate two murders at the same hotel. A bellboy is killed by a guest, leading the detectives to a case of kidnapping and counterfeiting. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sam investigates the background of an eastern European diplomat accused of a violent assault on a prostitute and learns he could be a murderer. The pressure is on when it emerges the suspect has full diplomatic immunity and is due to be deported within 24 hours. Phil and Grace interview a painter, whose valuable nude portrait of an ex-lover has been stolen. Leela accepts a post outside Sun Hill, unaware a colleague has pulled strings to ease her departure

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart works fast to find the children of separated parents when CCTV footage shows the brother and sister talking to a known paedophile. New recruit PC Benjamin Gayle is teamed with Will to investigate an assault on a Polish building worker and finds himself in a tight spot when the victim's colleagues cut up rough about his fish-poaching activities on the common. Micah Balfour stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart impresses his Sun Hill colleagues as he makes his debut on TV crime show Wanted, hoping to clear up a burglary case during which the victim, lawyer Ian Stroud, suffered a massive heart attack. The public response is overwhelming, leaving the team to pick through a mountain of false leads - including a sighting of a man once defended in court by Stroud who served eight years for a rape he did not commit

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A badly beaten woman found in possession of a bag full of passports claims to be an asylum seeker whose political activist husband was murdered in Zimbabwe. However, when her son is abducted, a completely different story emerges and the race is on to rescue the boy from a violent and vengeful father. Gina discovers a bank robber she believed had died in a bank vault blast years ago is alive and well - and back in the country. John Rogan and Nicky Henson guest star

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives of Nonnatus House return, with new nurse Barbara, who is anxious to prove herself - only to get off on the wrong foot after a late-night drinks party. A family of severely neglected children is discovered, so Trixie strives to restore their health and bring happiness into their lives, leaving Tom filled with admiration for the woman he loves, while Sister Evangelina finally undergoes tests for her abdominal pains. Shelagh finds she is at the surgery almost as much as she is at home, so the Turners come up with a solution which, for 1960, proves somewhat ground-breaking

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: An English tourist is strangled in her villa by a killer using the victim's scarf. The crime scene indicates a robbery gone wrong, but DI Poole recalls an almost identical case back in London. He grows even more suspicious when the victim's husband turns out to be crooked former colleague Doug Anderson, a name he had hoped was consigned to the past. But even though he has a watertight alibi and the team move on to further leads, Poole can't shake the notion that his old adversary is somehow involved. Ben Miller stars, with guest appearances from Neil Pearson, Ralf Little and Miller's former Primeval co-stars Hannah Spearritt and James Murray

programme.title: Zen
programme.desc: Detective drama, based on the best-selling novels by Michael Dibdin. Aurelio Zen begins to feel like he is the only honest policeman in Rome, as he sees the city becoming overrun by corruption and deceit - and is plunged into the thick of a political scandal when he is sent to re-investigate a murder in a remote village. As pressure increases from all sides, he finds himself longing for Tania Moretti, the Chief of Police's beautiful assistant - but matters become more complicated when a vengeful gangster starts hounding him. Drama, starring Rufus Sewell and Caterina Murino

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The body of a prostitute found in a river makes Ross realise an old adversary is importing girls from overseas to work in the vice trade. Unfortunately, his impetuous handling of the case puts others in danger and threatens to bring his career to an ignominious end - until he redeems himself with some quick thinking that gives the team a vital lead. Drama, starring Blythe Duff, John Michie and Colin McCredie

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh faces a dilemma when his friend Joey makes a dying wish, and the Knight family support Ajax and Meryl as they each confront a crisis

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh is shocked when he finds out Penny has accepted a job in the city, and the hospital is thrown into chaos

Meanwhile, Molly's prodigal brother returns, throwing himself on her mercy. Richard Briers, Alastair Mackenzie and Susan Hampshire star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey kisses Sally - strictly in the course of his duties - as they trail bank robber Vince Murray to find out what he's up to back in England. Meanwhile, the old lag's son Darren proves to be a chip off the old block, staging a burglary with Ian Forrest's lad Kieran, but it's news to the youngsters that Ian was killed in the bank vault explosion while Vince survived the blast. Guest starring Nicky Henson and John Rogan

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Paula goes missing, but knowing she is in urgent need of medical attention, Jac follows her, putting her own health in jeopardy by doing so. Concerned about the registrar, Michael tracks her down and is unable to stop her pursuing her mother to Bath, where years of secrets and lies are finally uncovered. Faye faces losing Linden when she learns she is pregnant - a discovery that reawakens her emotions after Archie's death

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Claire is snubbed by Luke as she tries to clear her brother's name, turning in desperation to his ex-girlfriend Zoe, who breaks down and reveals he also attacked her. Pete turns up at Bex's house and forces his way inside, and Stan is found injured on the street as his condition worsens

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When three teenagers collapse after taking uncut cocaine, Diane and Sally are sent to visit the school they all attend. There they break up a fight and further investigations reveal the two girls involved are both caught up in the local drugs scene as suppliers. Grace makes a momentous decision about her career and Phil drops a bombshell on his Sun Hill colleagues

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Everyone's talking about Mark but he isn't saying a word. Ruth and Pauline confront Peggy but she isn't giving an inch. Bianca learns a few home truths about Simon

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mark decides it's time to challenge people's prejudices. Ian and Cindy have a session with their counsellor, and Peggy has a hot date

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Paddy remains in hospital, and his friends are told that he may never recover his sight, while Marsha Stubbs' involvement with a local businessman leads to a brush with the law

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: A luncheon given by the area's new police caterers for local bigwigs is sabotaged, leaving Crabbe spoiled for choice when it comes to suspects. Detective drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan heads for trouble after castrating a pig and forgetting to dispose of the 'bits', and James provides some advice for a would-be bridegroom. Guest starring Thora Hird and Warren Clarke, with Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Lionel throws a party to celebrate Lesley's engagement - and his own business expansion - but is unaware that Veronica plans to leave him, Guest starring Janine Duvitski (Benidorm)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Bad-tempered Mr Grainger is deemed too long in the tooth to continue at Grace Brothers and calls are made for his resignation. Classic comedy, starring Arthur Brough and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy's dream concerning his flag finally comes to fruition, while Compo spots a romantic opportunity. Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: When Sid and Wally are seen disappearing into the woods, their wives Ivy and Nora wonder what they are up to, so press Compo, Clegg and Foggy to find out. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, written by Roy Clarke

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A young woman's body is washed up beside a pier, leading Gently and Bacchus to question the staff and guests of the nearby holiday camp where she worked. It turns out she was a popular member of staff, a charismatic performer and great with the guests - but her mother's reaction to her death makes the detectives suspect there is more to her family situation than meets the eye. And when her colleagues' alibis for the night of the murder don't quite stack up, a different picture of the victim - and the camp - comes to light. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with guest appearances from Pixie Lott, Lee Boardman, Lisa Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: New information links the unexplained death of a market trader to a series of drug rapes in east London - but with that case still ongoing, Ucos officers are unable to question the chief suspect, so they have to look into their victim's past in their search for clues. Gerry finds a likely culprit in the shape of the market inspector who clamped his car, while Sandra has problems of a more personal nature when her hated mother arrives for a visit. Sheila Hancock guest stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. When a woman defends her daughter from a mugger, she sets in motion a chain of events that places herself and her children in mortal danger, as her DNA is linked to an old murder case. Meanwhile, Boyd hears news of his son, who has been missing for seven years. Trevor Eve and Sue Johnston star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Mark feels frustrated at the power vacuum left by Vanessa's resignation as CEO, which has resulted in the porters going on strike. Connie is angry not to have been considered for the top job, but is distracted by the appearance of familiar faces from the past, who have arrived seeking her medical opinion, and Michael tries to get out of attending a marriage counselling session with Annalese. Guest starring Warren Donnelly (Stan in Shameless)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Selena's police surgeon shift with Will takes a dangerous turn when she is held hostage by a drug trafficker and forced to treat one of his couriers who has swallowed large amounts of heroin. Luke encourages Claire to talk to Bex, and Guppy fears he may have contracted hepatitis

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Former Footballers' Wives star Ben Richards joins the cast as PC Nate Roberts, a seasoned professional with an appetite for action and an eye for the ladies. Meanwhile, Phil Hunter clears his desk and prepares to move on, convinced none of his Sun Hill colleagues except Sam will give him a second thought. Diane is shocked to encounter an old army pal living in a squat with his girlfriend, who has been injured in a crack den fire

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Roy asks David to sort out a celebration meal for Pat. Carol is off with Alan, and Tony is uncomfortable in Simon's company. Mark's stall is quiet

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pat passes her driving test, and Joe storms out when he finds out why she was banned. Ben has a check up, Carol and Alan are still at odds and Simon asks Tony what's wrong

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: The regiment's tour in Germany is cut short. Meanwhile, a prank in the Officer's Mess causes trouble for Tucker. Starring Robson Green and Rob Spendlove

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Armed raiders rob wealthy guests at the restaurant, leaving Crabbe no option but to shut up shop. To make matters worse, hotshot DS Stringer is put in charge of the case, and soon finds himself unpopular with the staff by insulting Margaret and victimising Gary. Starring Richard Griffiths and Samantha Janus

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Tinker and Lovejoy set out to unravel the meaning behind a strange carving of a Japanese firefly cage, helped by Lady Jane Felsham. Drama, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Lionel struggles to cope without Veronica, and Jacko suspects that Sandra's shifty new boyfriend is taking advantage of their boss's low mood to help himself to the petty cash

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to move with the times by making an advertisement to be shown in local cinemas. Classic comedy, starring John Inman and the late Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy criticises local folklore and upsets Compo and Clegg by condemning a Yorkshire god called Earnshaw. However, subsequent events suggest he has been foolish to voice his opinions. Long-running comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Part one of two. Compo yearns to reach for the skies in a hang-glider to impress Nora Batty, but finds a challenge to climb a tree more than enough to handle. Meanwhile, the object of his affections pesters Wally to take her out for a meal. Long-running comedy set in rural Yorkshire, starring Bill Owen and Kathy Staff

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The poisoning of a Lancashire vicar appears to be a tragic accident until Lynley and Havers delve deeper into his past and discover a sinister motive for murder. Meanwhile, the detective's friendship with profiler Helen Clyde looks set to take on a new dimension. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of human remains in an urban canal is linked to the unsolved murder of a prison governor 15 years earlier, leading the team to seek out the original suspects. However, it is not long before the prison psychologist, who is married to the victim's daughter, finds himself personally embroiled in the investigation. Meanwhile, Boyd launches a search for his son Luke

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by foreign secretary Dominic Raab MP, shadow health secretary Jonathan Ashworth MP, leader of Plaid Cymru Adam Price MS and WHO technical lead on Covid-19 Maria Van Kerkhove. The newspapers are reviewed by BBC political correspondent Alex Forsyth and Daily Mirror political editor Pippa Crerar

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts moral and religious discussions on whether Britain is still racist, and would the world be a better place if everyone believed in reincarnation

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Ferris-Garbers yearn for the countryside on their doorstep and are hoping that a week in mum Caitlin's native Norfolk will convince their reluctant sons to leave London behind

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Ripon Cathedral to hear reflections on Lent, and Katie Piper meets a Christian in Leeds who donated a kidney to a stranger

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall where she sifts through the junk in search of two items that can be transformed. Upholsterer Ray Clarke transforms a traditional wing-back chair and there's a surprise in store with a rug Sarah found that could mean some cash to return to its unsuspecting owner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the recent meetings in Lievin and New York. With commentary by Steve Cram

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Bodnant Garden, a National Trust property near Tal-y-Cafn in Conwy, where items assessed include an ecclesiastical candlestick from a very special cathedral and some novelty glass bottles with a hefty price tag. There is also a tandem bicycle and sidecar and a selection of 20th-century automata

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Leeds United at Emirates Stadium, West Bromwich Albion v Manchester United at The Hawthorns, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St Mary's Stadium and Everton v Fulham at Goodison Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: A Royal Team Talk: Tackling Mental Health
programme.desc: The Duke of Cambridge, Gareth Southgate, Peter Crouch, Thierry Henry, Danny Rose and Jermaine Jenas gather together in the company of Dan Walker for an extraordinary and revealing conversation about men's mental health

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters visit a grand Edwardian home in Eltham, south-east London, a piece of land in Handsworth, Birmingham, and a property in Newcastle-under-Lyme in Staffordshire

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Donner Hegarty and husband Peter spend a trial week in New Zealand - the country of Peter's birth - hoping to make a decision on whether to move there permanently or stay in the UK

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A rural village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, and two paramedics with the Welsh Ambulance Service are victims of a terrifying attack in Bridgend

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles talks about jewellery with expert Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Tom tries to pull the wool over Rob's eyes, but Karen might be on to him. Lily needs a little help from her friends, and Zara tries to help a personal trainer with a problem

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: A German is found dead in a swimming pool during his stag party and Max and Miranda investigate whether it is a robbery gone wrong or was linked to his impending nuptials

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a newlywed Olympian and his wife look for a home in Wiltshire on a budget of £525,000, and he also tries his hand at the little-known sport of sticke tennis

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts. They begin by creating free-standing shelves using scaffolding poles and an old set of drawers

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy a vintage pedal car, a dentist's chair, a copper and brass desktop fan and a rickshaw

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is wary when Max approaches but to her surprise he offers an olive branch. However, Mick is clearly uncomfortable watching their exchange. Ash feels rejected when Iqra refuses to accept her apology, so she encourages Peter to help drown her sorrows

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Joel Dommett, Sindhu Vee, Alex Horne and AJ Odudu to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants form two groups for an homage to Eurovision in which they record and perform the same catchy song, trying to avoid the dreaded 'nul points'. Singer MNEK joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: A look at the contestants' lives during a seven-month break in filming due to the pandemic, featuring a combination of self-made video diaries and interview footage

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, while British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of developers hoping to transform their auction property buys in Devon, London and Wales

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A Belfast man wants to start a new life in New Zealand, but his wife and children are not convinced. A trial stay in Auckland sees the family check out what is on offer

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Two assaults cost a police officer his career and have a huge impact on his health as he is diagnosed with complex PTSD. In Kent, the drugs and gangs team are out on a raid

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Tim Weeks provide advice to two teams competing at the Royal Cornwall Showground near Wadebridge, while host Anita Manning explores Beer Quarry Caves in Devon

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al gets spooked, while Jimmi and Rob look into the Robson case. Sid gets caught between a father and daughter at odds over what's best for his patient

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the death of a famous DJ, and persuade a drug dealer to turn informant - but as the case unfolds, they begin to suspect that they are being used

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Surrey to find a new home in Norfolk, and visits a local public garden to help out with some pruning ahead of the grand opening

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy two mannequins, a Moorcroft vase, an electric massager, a piece of religious art and a curious ladder. With Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max is determined to get Linda back at any cost - but where does Phil fit into his plan? Jean encourages Sharon to give things another go with Phil, although Kat tells her she could do better. Iqra has shocking news for Martin, and after a heavy night's drinking, Peter turns superhero in his attempt to impress Ash

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's life is hanging in the balance, while Hanssen questions whether he did the right thing. Ange is devastated when she learns that things have not gone to plan, and Dom will face life-changing consequences of his injuries. Sacha vows to save Jodie's baby, and begins to understand the ongoing impact of her relationship with her mother. Cameron announces his intention to make a career change

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls. In a remote rural location, a small child falls in a loch, triggering a huge emergency response, while a motorway crash sees several people hospitalised, including a pregnant woman

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: Music's Dirty Secrets: Women Fight Back
programme.desc: Reporter Tamanna Rahman investigates how safe the music industry is for women, investigating the cases of a grime star currently serving 30 years in prison for rape, and a woman who shared her account of abuse and the hands of a rising rap star, leading to the cancellation of his debut album

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Weather forecaster Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, receiving inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order, and this programme features themes beginning with the letter A, and includes archive material from Gardeners' World, Countryfile and The Gardener's Year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on Crompton Moor in the southern Pennines to discover what makes this site of biological interest such a special place. Battered by rain, wind and snow, she learns how a plant is helping to restore these uplands, and meets a community coming together to bring wildlife back to the hills. Adam Henson tries out a new app to show how contented his animals are, wildlife film-maker Richard Taylor-Jones shows viewers how to spot wildlife on their doorsteps and Tom Heap investigates the mental health crisis in rural areas

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers have to protect their pups from a deadly enemy, while Mozart and her sister Kinkajoo are forced to depend on one another for survival. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Hannibal's son Wilson leaves the Commandos to go in search of a female to start a family of his own, but his loyalty is later tested when the Whiskers attack his old clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores the Scottish Borders by hot-air balloon, and discovers the role art and literature played in creating memorable gardens. She visits Abbotsford House, the former residence of writer Walter Scott, where she tries her hand at fly-fishing, and meets a woman whose family history at the property spans a century. She then visits Little Sparta in the Pentland Hills and examines a garden poem, before learning how the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh was created

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: On a route from Pwll to Kidwelly, Derek Brockway turns Pied Piper as he launches a walk of 800 children marking the anniversary of 'People Power' that saved a stretch of the Loughor Estuary for families to enjoy. On this Carmarthenshire coastal walk, the weatherman also discovers a former munitions factory that employed 6,000 people, has a spin in a racing car and learns the story Wales' feisty warrior princess Gwenllian

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how to make ingredients go just that little bit further, adding grated beetroot to make a tasty meatball supper and using a small garden harvest to create a pudding

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day seven of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring fourth-round singles matches at Melbourne Park

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Death (1946)
programme.desc: A Second World War bomber pilot survives jumping out of his stricken plane without a parachute and falls in love with the radio operator he had first spoken to during the fateful flight. A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The celebrities continue to settle into life in their 16th-century mansion, and are brought closer together when they celebrate Amanda Barrie's 81st birthday together. Lionel Blair - who has struggled with a distended belly since having treatment for cancer - tries out a local alternative medicine in the hope of finding a solution to the problem. Bill Oddie goes in search of wildlife with a trip into the jungle, and opens up about how spending time in nature has helped him cope with mental-health issues

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The fishing grounds of St Ives Bay have supported the fleets of two ports for generations, but now change threatens the future of the fishermen of Hayle. A vast new development with hundreds of homes and leisure facilities is under construction, and the fishermen fear that their days working from their compound next to it may be numbered

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second round of fixtures, featuring England v Italy at Twickenham, Scotland v Wales at BT Murrayfield and Ireland v France at Aviva Stadium. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Sam Warburton and Brian Moore

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the Alpine World Ski Championships from Cortina d'Ampezzo in Italy, including the men's and women's downhill races, as the fastest skiers in the world took on icy pistes and sheer gradients in their bids for gold

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The presenter discusses the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Mo Gilligan and Katherine Ryan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid

programme.title: Edie (2017)
programme.desc: Seeking to fulfil a decades-old dream to climb a mountain in the Scottish Highlands, Edith Moore, an 83-year-old widow, escapes to Scotland, where she grudgingly accepts the help of Jonny, the manager of a struggling shop that sells outdoor gear. Drama, starring Sheila Hancock and Kevin Guthrie

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan's ability is exposed and he loses control of the operation, forcing him to hide out in Mexico with Claire. Meanwhile, the Hunter spearheads the government's nefarious scheme and teams up with an unlikely partner to try to bring down all the heroes. Angela and Peter seek refuge in a church and begin to mend their broken relationship. Starring Adrian Pasdar, Hayden Panettiere, Cristine Rose and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: With the Hunter at the helm of the government operation, someone close to him is put in the line of fire, while Noah's marriage is pushed to breaking point. On their road trip, Hiro and Ando make a special delivery for Matt, and Angela unearths the demons of her past. Starring Zeljko Ivanek, Jack Coleman and Masi Oka

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha gets the shock of his life when a dangerous and vulnerable Jodie appears back on the scene. Kian's new boss Sahira breezes back into Holby, much to everyone's surprise. An outgoing new nurse has her first day working in a busy AAU, which proves to be a trial by fire

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles and experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique-hunting in Wrexham, before heading to Stockport for the auction. Eric is also faced with a mystery where the answer might be under his nose

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just as the annual lambing season gets into full swing, coronavirus grips the nation, and the farmers must work hard to keep their businesses afloat. In Aberdeenshire, new entrant farmers Isla and George lose their childcare, meaning Isla is left to look after the livestock and farm shop with three young children in tow. Elsewhere in the Lake District, Matt and Dani are faced with challenges when the usual team of students they rely on during their busiest time of year can no longer help with the calving

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as two teams put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection for a chance to win £1,000. In the first edition, Lynda and Kate from Bath take on Alice and James from the south coast of England as they go head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Sue

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Paul Hayes compete to buy the best lots at an Essex saleroom, each hoping his items raise the most money when they are sold

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ecologist Ellie Harrison explores Britain's most extreme winter landscapes, beginning with mountain terrain. Along the way, she reveals surprises that lie in store throughout the territory as she encounters seemingly out-of-place volcanos, avalanches and small herds of reindeer

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day eight of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring the concluding fourth-round singles matches at Melbourne Park

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: With the mob now numbering 50, the burrow is full to bursting point and Rocket Dog's leadership comes under threat. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Following the death of Whiskers leader Flower, Maybelline and Rocket Dog battle it out for dominance of their respective groups and strive to regain control over them. As winter approaches it looks as if the manor might not be big enough for both of them. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The team heads to Norwich Cathedral, where Thomas Plant and Kate Bateman unearth items including a religious relic and a Martin Brothers vase, and Paul Martin visits the circus

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Anna Richardson, Tom Rosenthal and Sindhu Vee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the final episode, the chef and the group of volunteers discover how much weight they have lost over the past two months, and how much fitter they are as a result of all their hard work. To celebrate their achievements, Tom decides to throw a special picnic by the river, and whips up a series of exciting, healthy recipes they can all enjoy

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Homer's Odyssey, The Good Life, the history of Portsmouth, and Taika Waititi, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Sliders take on the Puzzle Hunters in the second of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Macy's, San Pellegrino, Wagamama, and Heineken

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the sixth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Life in Cadgwith Cove, which is Cornwall's last traditional full-time fishing cove. For generations, sons have followed fathers off the beach and out to sea, and now the next generation is set to join the fleet. But while there might be new blood, the old working fabric of the cove itself is now under threat

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor, writer and comedian David Mitchell hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from November 2019, with stand-up Maisie Adam and former Conservative MP Rory Stewart joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the news

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Fall of an Icon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the Myanmar leader's fall from grace. While under house arrest during the country's military dictatorship, she became a Nobel prize-winning champion of democracy, human rights and peaceful protest. However, since becoming the leader of her nation, she has been implicated in brutal atrocities against the Rohingya Muslims, and faced allegations of genocide at the International Court of Justice. Key figures in international politics discuss the reasons why the qualities that brought her such acclaim have been in marked contrast to the realities of her leadership

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Tony Slattery was one of the most gifted TV comedians of the late 1980s and early 1990s, only to disappear from screens in 1996 amid rumours of a massive breakdown. As he approaches 60, he has been touring the country with a show that explores his past and his mental health. Although depression was diagnosed, he and his partner Mark Hutchinson are convinced he may have bipolar disorder, so Tony wants to have his mental health reassessed. But it will mean opening up about his addictions and a painful event in his childhood

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on Crompton Moor in the southern Pennines to discover what makes this site of biological interest such a special place. Battered by rain, wind and snow, she learns how a plant is helping to restore these uplands, and meets a community coming together to bring wildlife back to the hills. Adam Henson tries out a new app to show how contented his animals are, wildlife film-maker Richard Taylor-Jones shows viewers how to spot wildlife on their doorsteps and Tom Heap investigates the mental health crisis in rural areas

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist considers the rapidly changing language. Ian Hislop joins in a debate about the development of English due to texting and Twitter, which has led to a stand-off to grammar sticklers and language radicals. Mary also talks to musician Evelyn Glennie, who is profoundly deaf and communicates using touch and vibrations. Mary asks how life has changed for her at a time when everyone is communicating through cold, hard screens. Evelyn also presents an exclusive performance from Ely Cathedral

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles talks about jewellery with expert Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts. They begin by creating free-standing shelves using scaffolding poles and an old set of drawers

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Padstow is well known for its tourist industry, but there's another lucrative industry here that few visitors are aware of. Each year, Padstow's fishermen land hundreds of tonnes of brown crab. But the vast majority bypass the town altogether. For decades, lorryloads of shellfish have left Padstow for the continent every week. Once connected to Europe, Padstow's crabbing industry boomed. Britain's crab exports to Europe form an industry worth over sixty million pounds. But lockdowns across Europe have brought everything to a standstill, and as the global market for crab grows, concerns are starting to be raised about the effects of overfishing

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright shows us exercises designed to make the body strong

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Iain and Vikki from Loch Lomond take on Antonia and Charlie as they go head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Myk. Paul Martin hosts

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian battle for lots at a London auction house. While Jonty finds himself in a world of fashion, Danny muscles in on his territory

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lake District to explore how its landscape and wildlife are affected by Britain's changeable weather. During her journey, she encounters a rare Arctic winter resident and witnesses a lakeside spectacle performed by a family of otters

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day nine of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring the opening quarter-finals at Melbourne Park

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Wilson makes a move to join the Aztecs group, but when his advances are thwarted by Zaphod, he seeks the attention of a female from the Whiskers clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Nikita defends her land when the Whiskers cross into Commando territory

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Grand Pier at Weston-super-Mare in Somerset, where experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt pick out some gems to value. Among them are rare medals that once belonged to an early aviator and puppets that look like the Swedish band Abba. Plus, Paul delves into the history of seaside entertainment.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Anna Richardson, Tom Rosenthal and Sindhu Vee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with animals and keepers at Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park, beginning as a new arrival joins the resident dik-dik family. World Rhino Day gives staff a chance to let their hair down and have some fun as they raise both money and awareness of conservation issues

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform a country wedding venue in Sussex. Presented by Alan Carr with Judge Michelle Ogundehin

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old teenager is fatally stabbed in a park in Coventry, the forensics team must identify the murderers through evidence left behind at the crime scene. However, the weapon is nowhere to be found, so they are reliant on just two items - a mobile phone found a short distance from the body and the victim's blood-soaked jacket

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Mo Gilligan and Katherine Ryan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The presenter discusses the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Following on from the innovative series Your Home Made Perfect, Angela Scanlon returns with this show that offers the same hi-tech makeover technology to outdoor spaces. First up are Demi and Laura from Hertfordshire, who lead a busy family life, meaning they haven't had time to deal with their lacklustre, L-shaped plot. Garden designers Helen Elks-Smith and Manoj Malde pitch for the chance to redesign the site - demonstrating what they would do with virtual reality presentations that put the couple right at the heart of their new, improved outdoor havens. But which one will they choose?

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Victoria: A Royal Love Story
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce traces the monarch's love story by examining artefacts from a collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace. She looks at the paintings and sculptures she and Albert gave one another throughout their marriage, as well as meeting Prince Charles and visiting the couple's homes at Balmoral and on the Isle of Wight to understand more about their life together. Fiona also talks to the craftsmen restoring some of the objects today

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts Rome's rise from the abandonment and neglect of the 14th century into the everlasting seat of the Papacy recognised today. His story begins with the debauchery and decadence of the Renaissance, and takes viewers through the Catholic Reformation and on to the arrival of fascism and the creation of the Vatican State. He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: The team reaches Nepal, and journeys by tractor, local bus and dugout canoe to get to the Royal Chitwan National Park. Charley bonds with an elephant that takes him through remote Nepali villages, before reaching the capital Kathmandu. The adventurers also travel to Everest by helicopter and arrive in China, where they experience the breathtaking landscape of Yangshou from a hot-air balloon

programme.title: Britt-Marie Was Here (2019)
programme.desc: A sixtysomething former housewife starts work in the small town of Borg, where she is hired to coach the young soccer team. Comedy drama, starring Pernilla August, Vera Vitali and Peter Haber. In Swedish and German

programme.title: Love Songs at the BBC: A Valentine's Day Special
programme.desc: A selection of romantic songs for guilty pleasures, for courtship, for declarations of love, for looking someone in the eye and whispering sweet nothings, accompanied by a compilation of some of the greatest love songs from the likes of Celine Dion, Joe Cocker and Jennifer Warnes, Jason and Kylie, 10cc and Lionel Richie, all from the Top of the Pops era

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City

He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a secret little loft hidden in the woods, surrounded by evergreens and ruffled, green bushes and grass

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: A customised 1962 Chevy Impala has arrived at the Victoria & Albert for a new exhibition dedicated to cars. But this unique vehicle has come all the way from Los Angeles and as if that move was not tough enough, Catherine Sargent is now concerned it will be a tight fit getting such a large car into the exhibition space. Curator Alice Minter has chosen a table with a micro-mosaic top as part of an exhibition to tour the world, but it will need some serious restoration work before it can go anywhere, and a rare medieval jewel undergoes a deep clean

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a husband-and-wife team who revolutionised broadcast news in America by capturing breaking news stories in Los Angeles from their helicopter. Their cameras captured not only the adrenaline of live news culture, but also the strain it began to take on their relationship, which ultimately led to a major life transition for one of them

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: Documentary allowing viewers to immerse themselves in the lives of monks in three Benedictine monasteries, beginning with Downside Abbey in Somerset. It is home to 14 monks who live according to the sixth-century Rule of St Benedict and this episode follows two of the monks over the course of a typical, quiet monastic day, as they engage with carpentry and baking, religious services and moments of private prayer in the monastery gardens

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: Cameras capture the natural world through the perspectives of a cheetah, a turtle and an eagle, revealing their daily routines and the places that they call home in the wild

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Following master beadmaker Jane Semanto in her Nairobi studio as she crafts a traditional Maasai wedding necklace, which is of great ceremonial significance

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a secret little loft hidden in the woods, surrounded by evergreens and ruffled, green bushes and grass

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a countryside scene, complete with a big red barn and a rickety wooden fence

programme.title: The Golden Age of Canals
programme.desc: Documentary charting the changing face of Britain's canals after the Second World War, as enthusiasts fought to save the inland waterways from destruction - despite facing strong opposition from the government. Amateur and professional footage illustrates the canals' transformation from industrial thoroughfares to tourist attractions during the 1950s and 1960s, as well as efforts to restore and preserve the network's locks and boats. Narrated by Gina McKee

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: The second episode follows the a typical day in the life of the 21 monks residing in Pluscarden Abbey, a remote monastery on the edge of the Scottish Highlands in Moray. Given its isolated location, this Abbey is almost entirely self-sufficient, and the monks make their own clothes and grow their own crops, giving them little reason to have contact with the outside world

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby continues his voyage around Britain, sailing his yacht Rocket along coastal regions of the South East, the first point of attack for enemy forces and therefore essential to the nation's defence throughout history. Along the way, he takes a tour of HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship vessel from the Battle of Trafalgar, lovingly preserved in dry dock in Portsmouth. He also meets a carpenter who still makes wooden figureheads, discovers how a chamber pot acted as propaganda in the battle against Napoleon and reveals the way abstract art was used to deceive German submarine pilots

programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as two teams fight it out to dodge detention and put the cool back into school. In this episode, Mr Learner is training to be an art teacher and is working on his very own masterpiece. Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Harry and Ria, who are joined by CBBC’s Rhys Stephenson and comedians Lou Conran, Sam Fletcher and Bec Hill. They battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh. Rounds include Stick to the Point, the word association game where teams are put on the spot by Lauren and her stick of pointiness. If there is any hesitation or stumbling, Lauren will put them in their ‘shush’ positions. In new game Boxymorons, teams have to try and guess the object hidden inside a box. Unfortunately it clashes with a rare comet sighting, which Martha and Ollie are desperate to experience. They have even been allowed to borrow the very precious school telescope for the evening. Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. After much chaos and confusion, and with the presence of Ollie in the house rumbled, a compromise is finally reached, where the girls all get to do both the make-over and watch the rare comet. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. programme.desc: Stacey takes five teenagers to London Zoo for their first job on her ultimate work-experience challenge. If Sharif, Allina, Louis, Kieran and Louisa do well they will earn the national apprenticeship wage at the end of every day. However, if they fail to shine, they may go home empty-handed. All they have to do is spend two days working hard as a zoo keeper and impress their boss - what could possibly go wrong? The teens start off by doing two of the keepers’ regular daily tasks - feeding and cleaning out the animals - and it all has to be done thoroughly and quickly before the zoo opens to the public. When Matthew arrives at a park, he gets the chance to ride a steam train and find out how they make it go! We then follow Emily as she attends a Baby yoga class with her mum and sister. programme.title: Peter Rabbit

programme.title: What's On Your Head?

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: We help Kush find out all about buzzy, busy bumblebees! Ferne visits a wildflower garden to find different types of bees, while Rory and his son Sandy visit the park looking for bumblebees. They then return home to build their own bumblebee house. Plus, we find out how bumblebees help to make a third of all the food we eat by a process called pollination.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There is a turtle expert visiting the Safari World aquarium. Mr Hammond is on a scuba diving holiday in Borneo, so it’s up to Andy to ensure everything is ready for their arrival. Andy does a great job making the aquarium looking its best, but when Mr Hammond asks Andy if he has cleaned the green sea turtles, Andy is a little confused as he thought they cleaned themselves. Mr Hammond informs Jen and Andy that green sea turtles get clean by letting little fish nibble away the dirt and parasites on their skin and shell. However, he neglects to tell them what kind of fish, so Andy decides to find out for himself. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Andy blasts off through a magic portal in his safari-sub and heads to a beautiful coral reef off the coast of Bor
