programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join six-month-old Erin and Leo as they discover the world around them and start trying new foods for the first time. At home, we meet Erin and her family as everyone is playing and pretending to make phone calls on bananas. Meanwhile, Leo is playing on the swings at the local park, but soon it's time for his daily feed, while his sister stops to enjoy a delicious gingerbread man. After a short picnic, the family feeds the ducks and geese before Leo tries a rice cake for the very first time. At the petting farm, Erin and her family get the chance to feed some goats and hold a baby chick. The family have a picnic with juicy oranges and scrumptious sandwiches, while Erin has a bottle of milk. Leo’s family head home, where mum and Charlotte make a tasting platter for Leo, filled with fresh foods and a yummy yoghurt! At the dinner table, Erin’s mum dishes up a tasty meal - but will she like it?

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond is on a wildlife trip in New Zealand, and Andy is eager to hear what animals he has spotted. Jen and Andy call Mr Hammond from the Aqua Base, who answers their call standing in a river, wearing full fishing gear. He informs Jen and Andy that he has seen killer whales and is fishing for trout. Suddenly, there is a barking noise and Mr Hammond looks in disbelief as he sees a fur seal pup disappear into the forest. Andy reassures a distressed Mr Hammond that he and Jen will investigate why the seal pup is making its way into the forest. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the south island of New Zealand.

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. programme.title: Panorama

Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Surrey to find a new home in Norfolk, and visits a local public garden to help out with some pruning ahead of the grand opening

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy two mannequins, a Moorcroft vase, an electric massager, a piece of religious art and a curious ladder. With Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas bring you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max is determined to get Linda back at any cost - but where does Phil fit into his plan? Jean encourages Sharon to give things another go with Phil, although Kat tells her she could do better. Iqra has shocking news for Martin, and after a heavy night's drinking, Peter turns superhero in his attempt to impress Ash

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's life is hanging in the balance, while Hanssen questions whether he did the right thing. Ange is devastated when she learns that things have not gone to plan, and Dom will face life-changing consequences of his injuries. Sacha vows to save Jodie's baby, and begins to understand the ongoing impact of her relationship with her mother. Cameron announces his intention to make a career change

programme.title: Nightshifters
programme.desc: Stuart has been working night shifts at Tata Steelworks in Port Talbot for almost 20 years, Sian is on the late-night taxi shift in Swansea, while in Cardiff, artist Nathan is melting chocolate in a microwave prior to creating a portrait of Lord Alan Sugar using sweets and confectionery. In this continuing series revealing the hidden world of night-time workers, all three reveal their very different reasons for working at night

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: Music's Dirty Secrets: Women Fight Back
programme.desc: Reporter Tamanna Rahman investigates how safe the music industry is for women, investigating the cases of a grime star currently serving 30 years in prison for rape, and a woman who shared her account of abuse and the hands of a rising rap star, leading to the cancellation of his debut album

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew Marr is joined by foreign secretary Dominic Raab MP, shadow health secretary Jonathan Ashworth MP, leader of Plaid Cymru Adam Price MS and WHO technical lead on Covid-19 Maria Van Kerkhove. The newspapers are reviewed by BBC political correspondent Alex Forsyth and Daily Mirror political editor Pippa Crerar

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts moral and religious discussions on whether Britain is still racist, and would the world be a better place if everyone believed in reincarnation

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Aftab Gohar of Abbotsgrange Parish Church in Grangemouth and the Rev Sarah Shaw of Christ Church in Falkirk present the religious programme

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters visit a grand Edwardian home in Eltham, south-east London, a piece of land in Handsworth, Birmingham, and a property in Newcastle-under-Lyme in Staffordshire

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Ripon Cathedral to hear reflections on Lent, and Katie Piper meets a Christian in Leeds who donated a kidney to a stranger

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall where she sifts through the junk in search of two items that can be transformed. Upholsterer Ray Clarke transforms a traditional wing-back chair and there's a surprise in store with a rug Sarah found that could mean some cash to return to its unsuspecting owner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the recent meetings in Lievin and New York. With commentary by Steve Cram

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Bodnant Garden, a National Trust property near Tal-y-Cafn in Conwy, where items assessed include an ecclesiastical candlestick from a very special cathedral and some novelty glass bottles with a hefty price tag. There is also a tandem bicycle and sidecar and a selection of 20th-century automata

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Leeds United at Emirates Stadium, West Bromwich Albion v Manchester United at The Hawthorns, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St Mary's Stadium and Everton v Fulham at Goodison Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of St Johnstone v Celtic at McDiarmid Park, plus a round-up of Saturday's matches in the Scottish Premiership

programme.title: A Royal Team Talk: Tackling Mental Health
programme.desc: The Duke of Cambridge, Gareth Southgate, Peter Crouch, Thierry Henry, Danny Rose and Jermaine Jenas gather together in the company of Dan Walker for an extraordinary and revealing conversation about men's mental health

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles and experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique-hunting in Wrexham, north Wales, before heading to Stockport for the auction. Eric is also faced with a mystery where the answer might be under his nose

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Donner Hegarty and husband Peter spend a trial week in New Zealand - the country of Peter's birth - hoping to make a decision on whether to move there permanently or stay in the UK

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A rural village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, and two paramedics with the Welsh Ambulance Service are victims of a terrifying attack in Bridgend

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing, with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Tom tries to pull the wool over Rob's eyes, but Karen might be on to him. Lily needs a little help from her friends, and Zara tries to help a personal trainer with a problem

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: A German is found dead in a swimming pool during his stag party and Max and Miranda investigate whether it is a robbery gone wrong or was linked to his impending nuptials

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a newlywed Olympian and his wife look for a home in Wiltshire on a budget of £525,000, and he also tries his hand at the little-known sport of sticke tennis

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts. They begin by creating free-standing shelves using scaffolding poles and an old set of drawers

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents as five dealers bid against one another to buy a vintage pedal car, a dentist's chair, a copper and brass desktop fan and a rickshaw

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda is wary when Max approaches but to her surprise he offers an olive branch. However, Mick is clearly uncomfortable watching their exchange. Ash feels rejected when Iqra refuses to accept her apology, so she encourages Peter to help drown her sorrows

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Joel Dommett, Sindhu Vee, Alex Horne and AJ Odudu to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The contestants form two groups for an homage to Eurovision in which they record and perform the same catchy song, trying to avoid the dreaded 'nul points'. Singer MNEK joins Michelle Visage and Graham Norton on the judging panel

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: A look at the contestants' lives during a seven-month break in filming due to the pandemic, featuring a combination of self-made video diaries and interview footage

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, while British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

Jean encourages Sharon to give things another go with Phil, although Kat tells her she could do better. Iqra has shocking news for Martin, and after a heavy night's drinking, Peter turns superhero in his attempt to impress Ash

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's life is hanging in the balance, while Hanssen questions whether he did the right thing. Ange is devastated when she learns that things have not gone to plan, and Dom will face life-changing consequences of his injuries. Sacha vows to save Jodie's baby, and begins to understand the ongoing impact of her relationship with her mother. Cameron announces his intention to make a career change

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls. In a remote rural location, a small child falls in a loch, triggering a huge emergency response, while a motorway crash sees several people hospitalised, including a pregnant woman

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Investigations into social and political issues affecting Northern Ireland

programme.title: Collaborations - with Stephen McCauley
programme.desc: Musicians from various genres team up for one-off performances of new or reversioned songs. Mixing it up are And So I Watch You From Afar, joined by Ríoghnach Connolly, Declan O'Rourke teaming up with hip-hop producer Cbakl, the Gloaming's Iarla Ó Lionáird joining forces with Úna Monaghan and West Ocean String Quartet, and Saint Sister and Lisa Hannigan sharing a state together once more. Presented by Stephen McCauley

programme.title: Music's Dirty Secrets: Women Fight Back
programme.desc: Reporter Tamanna Rahman investigates how safe the music industry is for women, investigating the cases of a grime star currently serving 30 years in prison for rape, and a woman who shared her account of abuse and the hands of a rising rap star, leading to the cancellation of his debut album

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.desc: Questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly to the Minister for Justice, Naomi Long, from Monday 8 February

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Questions in the Northern Ireland Assembly to the Minister for Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, Gordon Lyons, from Monday 8 February

programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to France's Europe Minister, Clément Beaune. The European Union faces a huge Covid challenge. The vaccine roll out has been slow, internal free movement is a concern and tensions with Britain post-Brexit have risen. Is the virus exposing weaknesses in the EU?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Exiles: Hong Kong at a Crossroads
programme.desc: In June 2020, after months of increasingly violent protests in Hong Kong, Beijing imposed a sweeping national security law. Critics say it effectively curtails protest and freedom of speech - China has said it will return stability. In response, the UK has introduced a new visa that will give 70% of its former colony's population - 5.4m people - the right to live in the UK, and eventually become citizens. Many activists now face an agonising choice - to stay or to go.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: With schools, colleges and universities around the world closed by Covid-19, the pandemic is blowing a hole in the lives of young people, their education and their life prospects. Remote learning can accentuate the differences between the haves and the have-nots. Time out of school can also mean a lack of decent food for the poorest and increase the risk of domestic violence against girls. Higher education has been thrown into disarray, with those on the cusp of graduation entering an uncertain job market. How can we stop this cruel disruption to young lives becoming a global generational disaster?

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.desc: Sid wants to give Penny a present

programme.desc: Everybody is really excited today as, while building a playhouse, Bing, Sula and Pando find a bird’s nest with eggs inside

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is not at all happy when Pando calls him 'Biongalaidh Bongalaidh' all the time but after a while, he learns that songs with silly words are fun sometimes

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Shifu's father visits the Jade Palace, but Po is disappointed to learn that he and his son do not get along

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Everything goes like clockwork until Coinneach Clogaid arrives on the scene - and the cuckoo agrees with this wholeheartedly

programme.desc: Mata and his robbers are trying to drive Borca and his men out of the fort and Ronia ia surprised that she hasn’t seen Biorc for quite some time

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Sìm is deaf, but he never lets that get in the way of mischief making, and he's delighted to be spending the holidays with his granny

programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, Glasgow Gaelic Musical Association sing Molaibh Dia, and Niall MacLennan and Alasdair MacPhee perform Psalm 96, Isabel MacLeod shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Annabel Maclennan presents a selection of Eòrpa reports from previous years connected to Europe's coasts and seas. The programme includes an account of how migrants make the perilous sea crossing to Greece to seek a better life, a report on a controversial plan to build a canal near Poland's border with Russia, and how record low water levels in the River Danube were affecting Hungary's tourism industry

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: Martin prepares hot cross buns, and in the restaurant in Balivanich, the family pull together as they prepare for a very important annual event - Mother's Day

programme.title: A' Suathadh Ri Iomadh Rubha
programme.desc: On his death in 2015, Norman Campbell had written published many novels and children's books in Gaelic. He remains one of the 20th century's most respected Gaelic writers. This documentary reveals not just details of Norman's work, but also that of his father and uncle - and of the achievements of his children in the arts

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy presents a show with folk and traditional music from Celtic Connections. This programme features a cross section of music, including Kris Drever and band, Dallahan, Rhiannon Giddens and Josie Duncan and her band

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Ingrid plans a party for Fredrik's 50th birthday and breaks social rules to support a friend in trouble. Christian and the other divers at the Ocean Viking are taking risks every day at work, where things are getting increasingly dangerous. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Kris Drever's solo performance, including ‘Freedom Come A' Ye', from Na Trads in 2019

programme.desc: The Cuttlefish tells how us how they can change their colour in a split second so that they can hide from predators

programme.title: Baile Mhuilinn
programme.desc: Gaelic songs and stories for children who are just learning to read, featuring the letter C

programme.title: A'Bhuth (Early Stages Mathematics)
programme.desc: Calum uses the two-times table to count his badges

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Siobhan MacInnes reads Air an t-Slighe Dhachaigh, written by Jill Murphy and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: The honourable judge Papa Mùman presides over matters when a trial takes place in the garden – The Case of Rudeigin and Rob versus the Gràisg

programme.title: Port Pàipeir
programme.desc: Charlie plays a joke on Matilda, replacing her chocolate-flavour milk for instant coffee. Matilda spends the night awake and due to her power, dawn never comes

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to eavesdrop on friends using a super spy listening device and bend a ball like David Beckham

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Coinneach Clogaid, a rabbit, a carrot and a little magic - what a combination

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: Iain Maclean deals with two calving cows in Caithness, Colin Scott tends to a lion at Blair Drummond and Alison Cheshire operates on a bulldog's eye at Ardene

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: The Empire State Building, including working at height. Plus, the visit of Winston Churchill

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra na h-Alba
programme.desc: Murdo Macdonald visits some of the country's most important gardens to explore their design, plant-life, setting and history, beginning with Gordon Castle Walled Garden

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: A nostalgic look back at going to school, beginning with personal accounts of the first day, when children leave their parents' side for the first time in their lives

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads talk to their local ladies' rugby team and prepare some after-match food on the touchline at one of their games

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod is in Geirinis sorting out a problem with rising damp. Florence is considering demolishing her garden shed as all the material she stores is soaking wet because of moisture coming through the walls. After clearing the space, Donnie can get cracking on his planned solution of a raised floor. The presenter also visits Cothrom ReStore in Ormacleit and tries his hand at upcycling furniture and the work continues on the Caladh Trust kitchen renovation in Benbecula

programme.title: Sgeul Slainte
programme.desc: A group of nurses gathers to discuss the job, share anecdotes and reflect on how the profession has changed since the Second World War

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: Allan MacDonald introduces performances by Aoife Scott & Andy Meaney, I Draw Slow and the Dublin Lasses from the Dublin music festival

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Many villagers have a cold and are feeling extremely unwell

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon reads Seanmhair Eun, written by Benji Davies and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: It's games week in Uist and the bakers are busy preparing rolls that are much needed for the hundreds who will be heading to the machair both in the North and the South

programme.title: Macleod MP/Maclean KGB
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald reveals the forces that shaped the lives of Soviet spy Donald Maclean and Tory cabinet minister Iain Macleod

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: Two tourists visiting the community decide to see what the local museum has to offer

programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, Glasgow Gaelic Musical Association sing Molaibh Dia, and Niall MacLennan and Alasdair MacPhee perform Psalm 96, Isabel MacLeod shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.desc: George wins tickets to the local stately home. It's not long before Dave and the rest of the Spuds find themselves lost in its maze.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Summer rears its hot, cheeky head in Grimsby, but Dave has found a way to keep the Spud flat nice and cool

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A former disaster specialist comes out of retirement to help Virgil and Gordon cap a leaking gas reserve

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's dare is to drive a tank blindfolded. Can he get across the course and knock down the cardboard box wall?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes on a date with his girlfriend, complete with romantic movie and candlelit dinner

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Following a bump to the head, Mercury seems to be acting like a dog, much to Mike's surprise

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Batgirl's worlds collide when her best friend from Gotham City moves to Metropolis.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter hikes seven and a half miles of the wild Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall's most southerly point, starting at the much-photographed Kynance Cove and finishing at the idyllic fishing village of Cadgwith. Along the way she learns about Serpentine Rock, visits a hut that changed the course of modern technology, and gets a pasty making master class

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh is joined by Wendy Craig, Will Kirk and Frank Gardner

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team heads to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Despite Jim's own diagnosis, his commitment to spreading awareness and raising money for prostate cancer charities are legendary in the area, including creating a 'Calendar Girls' style naked calendar. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). Jill Douglas presents coverage of the final match in the second round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. France overcame Ireland 35-27 in Paris in the concluding contest of last year's competition, but were unable to secure the bonus point and 31-point margin required to prevent England winning the title, while Ireland could have topped the table had they recorded a bonus and prevailed by seven points or more. With analysis by Brian O'Driscoll, Rory Best and Gareth Thomas, and commentary by Miles Harrison, David Flatman and Geordan Murphy

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: This week it's The Greatest Love Stories On Ice as the remaining celebrities and their professional partners battle to stay in the competition. Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield host, while Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Plus, a guest performance by Rita Ora, who performs her new single, Bang Bang

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Host Jeremy Clarkson may also lend a hand, if the players trust his general knowledge

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice decides to reject Tanvi's offer - Harry would have wanted Alice to develop the land herself with the support of their family. But it's not long before the land project is overshadowed, as Nicola blurts out that Alice is also hoping to try for Harry's baby using the sperm that he mysteriously stored before he died. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo continue their European adventure in Italy, starting in the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they'll be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. The next day Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat, before the Walshes are invited to the home of opera superstar Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Claire Martin was abandoned in Hong Kong as a baby more than 50 years ago and heads back there to appeal for information about her origins, but with the help of DNA she finds family much closer to home. The team helps Nicki Goscomb and her sisters search for their brother, who was given up for adoption in 1966

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Married couple Sandeep and Judd, Wirral sisters Nicola and Pamela, and Tamworth couple Karen and Ben use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing. Hosted by Adil Ray

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Croft, featuring Round seven of the Porsche Sprint Challenge and round 14 of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Willie, Sarah, Harry and Christine pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped, Diane offers advice, and Manpreet is crestfallen

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi remains oblivious to Kevin's fate, Tyrone's recent blunder causes heartache at Number 9, and Johnny receives a diagnosis for his condition

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo embark on the final leg of their European road trip as they explore more of Italy, engaging in gladiatorial combat in Rome, before continuing south and making a stop to soak up the views from a sky-high zipwire. As they reach their final stop on the Amalfi Coast, they get the chance to swim in the sea, but they'll have to dive in from a cliff

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi vows to track down a missing Kevin, Simon accuses Leanne of wasting money, and Billy takes Todd's good deed at face value

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives, following a woman on a quest to solve a family mystery and find her sisters, and a woman living with the heart-breaking decision to give up her son for adoption when she was just a teenager

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: Starting in the Outer Hebrides, Joanna explores the Harris tweed industry, and drives a herd of Highland cattle across the sands at low tide to their winter grazing. Next she heads to Eilean Donan Castle, the location for her first episode of The New Avengers. Crossing to Northern Ireland, Joanna visits the Dark Hedges, made famous in Game of Thrones, before having a laugh with one of the Derry Girls. She then meets the inspiring founder of the Children in Crossfire charity

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Josie d'Arby, Emilia Fox, Richard Madeley and John Thomson to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall meets a woman hoping to track down the sister her mother was forced to give up almost 50 years ago. Plus, a youngster desperate to get back to the sport he loves following an allergic reaction which robbed him of his eyesight, and a man determined to give back to the NHS following a life-saving operation

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Farooq, Neil, Carys and Emily, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is determined, Manpreet tries to reach out to Rishi, and Charles tries to convince Rishi

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet. The final episode takes Joanna from North Wales to St Michael's Mount and the West Country, ending in her home city of London

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: The detective investigates the truth about Bobby. Trying to track down her own stalker leads to a shocking truth. Stacey attempts to escape the family with her baby. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers welcomes Gemma Collins for an emotional, surprising and hilarious interview. The queen of reality recounts how she was bullied as a teenager, suffered a miscarriage and has experienced mental health problems. She also talks about living with fellow The Only Way is Essex star Arg and how she spends the money she has made

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Following an unexpected turn in the case, Lisa and the team struggle to keep their heads in the game as they are forced to accept help from a new unit. As one suspect throws new light on the investigation, Lisa doubts whether she has been given the full truth on events from the past. With the investigative pressure building, Manning attempts to fight for his marriage, and Lisa finds solace in a place she least expected

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: Starting in the Outer Hebrides, Joanna explores the Harris tweed industry, and drives a herd of Highland cattle across the sands at low tide to their winter grazing. Next she heads to Eilean Donan Castle, the location for her first episode of The New Avengers. Crossing to Northern Ireland, Joanna visits the Dark Hedges, made famous in Game of Thrones, before having a laugh with one of the Derry Girls. She then meets the inspiring founder of the Children in Crossfire charity

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Staff at the London hotel prepare for Valentine's Day, one of the busiest and most profitable nights of the year. Restaurant Director Thierry is still reeling from owner Gordon Ramsay's visit to the Grill, but he gets to work re-training his front-of-house staff and looking at how he can improve his already high standards. Downstairs in the florists, boss Belinda and junior florist MaryKate prepare £20,000 worth of flowers, which will be decorated across the hotel throughout the week

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. programme.desc: Village newcomer Vivienne Mackay is found battered to death in her own kitchen after announcing her intention to marry businessman Dominic Bellingham - who has not yet separated from his current wife. PC Mason is reminded of his own mother's death, and personal feelings threaten to cloud his professional judgement. Dawn seizes an opportunity to make a fast buck using Vince's hot dog van on the day of a big football match but finds herself in cut-throat competition with Peggy and her baked potato operation. Nikki Sanderson and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy vows to do all he can to get Mandy back. Mrs Kirk, however, is intent on sabotaging their relationship. Betty and Jack go to visit Kathy in hospital

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is convinced that Kathy is on the mend. Mrs Kirk offers Mandy money to stay away from Paddy. Steve is worried whether Kathy will remember the accident

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred decides to get on with his life and forget Maureen. Mavis bids an emotional farewell as she leaves for Cartmel. Deirdre goes to the airport to confront Jon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon tries his best to explain to Deirdre why he lied. Emily receives a shock when she finds something inappropriate in Ken's house. Maureen instructs Maud to sell the shop

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist dies of a heart attack shortly after smashing the seal of an ancient Egyptian tomb, raising the spectre of the mummy's curse. The grieving widow summons the Belgian sleuth to unravel the mystery, but he can only smoke out the real villain by risking his own life. David Suchet stars, with Anna Cropper and Bill Bailey

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are not keen on moving to a council estate in Leeds. Steve cannot sleep as he worries about what Kathy remembers. Mandy tells the Dingles about Mrs Kirk's offer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak compiles a list of eligible bachelors for Mandy to marry. Kim is angry at Steve for attacking Chris. Terry thinks it is his lucky night with Tricia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre apologises to Ken for doubting him. Leanne and Toyah are amused to hear their house may be haunted. Rita struggles to cope on her own at the Kabin

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon promises Deirdre he will not blow his second chance with her. Ashley, Gail and Martin attend Don's funeral. Judy starts work at the Kabin, with Katie in the back room

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Leanne lies to Nick, making out that Imran's keeping her job open for her and she is fine for money. Having failed to secure a mortgage, David and Shona are at their wit's end. Johnny is horrified when he fails to recognise a cry for help from Joe, a suicidal fellow prisoner

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Gabby shows potential to Kim, Noah is annoyed by Charity's interference, and Matty and Amy arrange a date

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Staff at the London hotel prepare for Valentine's Day, one of the busiest and most profitable nights of the year. Restaurant Director Thierry is still reeling from owner Gordon Ramsay's visit to the Grill, but he gets to work re-training his front-of-house staff and looking at how he can improve his already high standards. Downstairs in the florists, boss Belinda and junior florist MaryKate prepare £20,000 worth of flowers, which will be decorated across the hotel throughout the week

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. It seems like an open-and-shut case - but Lane badly mishandles the entire investigation, forcing Wycliffe and Kersey to intervene. Jack Shepherd stars as the Cornish copper

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks to the future following an attempt on his life, and while he weighs up his options, DCI Lane resolves to pursue her lofty ambitions. Meanwhile, DI Kersey investigates a salmon poacher's suspicious death. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Jimmy Yuill and Helen Masters

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Sophie, the wife of one of Kersey's friends, goes missing after an evening class and soon her tutor is found murdered. The detectives step in to investigate but the clues lead to standing stones linked to the entrance to hell in local superstition. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers where the money for his birthday party came from. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.desc: Jeff and Meg go to California in search of high-calibre cars to bring to the prestigious Auburn Auction in Indiana

programme.title: Auto Mundial
programme.desc: The latest news and reviews from the world of cars, featuring the Porsche Panamera and a crossover from Kia

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents a action from Croft, featuring Round seven of the Porsche Sprint Challenge and round 14 of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Jager family boat hits the water after being grounded for four months. Skipper Glenn needs money, and he's taking his son with him, but a predator puts their haul in doubt. Plus, Squizzy is on a four-day run to restock his mobile lobster van, but rough seas and the market crisis leave him emotional

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The concrete boat job has turned into a nightmare. The houseboat has collapsed in on itself and there's mess and concrete debris everywhere. While Luke, Jayde and Ellie navigate the extreme humidity, mud and mangroves, Dan and Jordan are off to evaluate their next job. The Belle Canto is smashed upon the rocks. It's a total write-off and they wont be able to use Christopher to winch it out. To make matters worse, Jordan has lost control of the team's dingy.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Moriarty seeks revenge on Holmes for foiling his plan to sell the Mona Lisa, and the pair ultimately fight their final battle at the Reichenbach Falls in Switzerland. Jeremy Brett stars as the titular hero, with David Burke making his final appearance as Dr Watson and Eric Porter as Moriarty

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes

programme.desc: Britain's most advanced computer suffers a series of acts of sabotage. Steed and Tara try to decode a cryptic message from the machine naming the culprit. Dennis Price guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole's Mediterranean odyssey continues with a ride through southern Spain. Back home, the restoration of the 1970 Chopper gathers pace, and Henry also gets a sneak peak at the latest models from AJS

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A girl is followed home from a launderette and abducted. The man responsible seems to want Makepeace find him, which concerns Dempsey.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed finds himself having to trust agent Melville, who has lost three partners and seems to be lying constantly, as he attempts to convict Lord Edgefield of blackmail. Patrick Macnee stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Boxer Lewis Nelson hires Ray to keep his gambling-addicted wife away from the casino he partly owns. However, she persuades Arthur to play the roulette wheels on her behalf, tempting him with the 'winning system' she has devised. Comedy starring George Cole

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members from Lincoln answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The team drives to Kalgoorlie, the gold mining capital of Australia, to salvage a 10-tonne gold dry-blower that has been sitting idle for months and is costing its owner hundreds of thousands of dollars in lost revenue. With the price of gold skyrocketing, the owner wants to get it repaired and back out onto the goldfields. The team has one day and a budget of 20,000 dollars to retrieve the money-making machine.

programme.title: Cobra (1986)
programme.desc: A maverick Los Angeles police officer is assigned to the case of a serial killer and becomes convinced the murders are not the work of just one man but an organised gang of psychopaths. His theory turns out to be true and he has to battle to protect the sole surviving witness to their reign of terror. Action thriller, with Sylvester Stallone, Brigitte Nielsen and Brian Thompson

programme.title: Undercover Police: Hunting Paedophiles
programme.desc: Following a middle-aged male undercover officer as he assumes a new identity as a 13-year-old girl from Essex and begins engaging online with people who have demonstrated a sexual interest in children. Within minutes of creating an account on a social media site for teenagers, 'Keira' is bombarded with highly sexualised messages from other users, many of whom are adult men

programme.title: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: When elderly teacher Peter Farquhar was found dead at home in the village of Maids Moreton in 2015, it was sad and unexpected, but no one thought it suspicious. But 18 months later, when his neighbour Ann Moore-Martin also fell ill and died, the police decided to look again. They found that Ben Field, a would-be vicar and academic, had recently become part of both of their lives and benefited from their wills. This programme shows that by delving back in time through friends' and loved ones' memories of Peter and Ben's relationship, as well as Peter's diary, the police discovered a story of love and betrayal

programme.title: Confessions of a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Samuel Little claims to have killed up to 93 women across various US states over four decades, with the FBI confirming they have good reason to believe that he was responsible for over half of those murders. Travelling from California to Mississippi, film-maker Ben Zand tries to get to the bottom of how such a prolific killer was on the loose for so long and why the victims were allowed to pile up, seemingly unnoticed

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take That's Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness

programme.title: Liberty
programme.desc: Niels's idealism is tested to the limit when it comes to preserving the livelihood of Tanscan's workers, while Kirsten is heading for new horizons. In Danish

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: The series following extraordinary singletons as they search for romance returns. Nicholas, a 26-year-old from Berkshire who has Asperger's, longs to find a partner to share his life with. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Shantae, who has Down's Syndrome, goes to the Pentecostal church every Sunday with her family and dreams of meeting a religious family man. Cameras also follow Robbie Williams superfan Sam. He is 28, lives in Devon and has a global learning delay. He's a successful horse rider, competes regularly and has won scores of rosettes. Can he find Ms Right?
